mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and increases discount rates, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of CPIAUCSL on 60-day forward returns remains above -0.02 over any 3-year period, or inflation rising >0.3% monthly is followed by positive returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below full-employment thresholds, labor market tightness fuels wage-cost pass-through and tighter monetary policy expectations, depressing equity prices.
failure_condition: In the 3 years after a UNRATE drop below 5%, the 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the average return exceeds +0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with rising policy rates signals that the economy is overheating and the central bank will continue tightening, which dampens equity risk appetite and forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 3% and FEDFUNDS > previous quarter average
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDP growth × FEDFUNDS change) fails to produce a negative coefficient with p < 0.10 in a multivariate regression on 60-day returns over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts precedes broad industrial production strength, and the joint acceleration of both real activity proxies signals sustained economic momentum that boosts equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month increase > 2% and INDPRO increase > 0.5% in same month
failure_condition: When both triggers are active, the 5-day forward SPX return is negative or the signal precision (positive returns / total triggers) stays below 55% over a 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, a rise in retail sales signals the onset of a demand recovery before labor market healing is complete, which is rewarded by equity markets as a leading indicator of economic turnaround.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6% and RSAFS rises > 2% month-over-month, the 20-day forward SPX return is not positive in at least 60% of such episodes over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment gains above a well-established threshold increase expectations of imminent labor market tightening and eventual Fed action, which weighs on short-term equity returns as rate hike fears intensify.
failure_condition: Following months with PAYEMS gains > 200k, the 5-day forward SPX return shows no negative average or is positive over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect real economic output expansion and rising corporate earnings, which directly support higher equity prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO monthly changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is negative or below 0.05 over any 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and a very tight labor market indicates that consumer demand is overheating, which forces the central bank to tighten in a way that depresses equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition (RSAFS YoY > 5% and UNRATE < 5%) fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a success ratio above 55% across any 4-year sample.
